name: Advanced npm Scripts with Pre/Post Hooks
description: Automate build, test, and deployment workflows using npm scripts with lifecycle hooks, cross-platform commands, and script composition.

steps.title: Define common scripts
steps.description: Set up build, test, and development scripts in package.json.
steps.command: npm pkg set scripts.dev='next dev' scripts.build='next build' scripts.start='next start' scripts.test='jest --coverage' scripts.lint='eslint . --fix'

steps.title: Add pre and post lifecycle hooks
steps.description: Run tasks automatically before or after standard scripts.
steps.command: npm pkg set scripts.prebuild='npm run lint && npm run test' scripts.postbuild='echo Build completed at $(date)'
steps.tip: Pre/post hooks run automatically. 'prebuild' runs before 'build', 'postbuild' runs after. This works for any script name.

steps.title: Compose scripts with npm-run-all
steps.description: Run multiple scripts in parallel or sequentially.
steps.command: npm install -D npm-run-all2 && npm pkg set scripts.validate='run-s lint test build' scripts.dev:all='run-p dev:api dev:web'
steps.tip: run-s runs scripts sequentially (stops on failure). run-p runs them in parallel.

steps.title: Use environment variables in scripts
steps.description: Pass configuration to scripts via environment variables.
steps.command: npm pkg set scripts.build:prod='NODE_ENV=production npm run build' scripts.test:ci='CI=true npm test -- --reporters=default --reporters=jest-junit'
steps.warning: Setting env vars inline like NODE_ENV=production works on macOS/Linux but not Windows. Use cross-env for cross-platform compatibility.

steps.title: List and run available scripts
steps.description: View all defined scripts and understand what is available.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# npm Script Automation Setup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Current Scripts ==="
npm run 2>/dev/null || echo "No scripts defined"

echo ""
echo "=== Setting Up Standard Scripts ==="

# Core scripts
npm pkg set scripts.dev='next dev'
npm pkg set scripts.build='next build'
npm pkg set scripts.start='next start'
npm pkg set scripts.lint='eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx --fix'
npm pkg set scripts.test='jest --coverage'
npm pkg set scripts.typecheck='tsc --noEmit'

# Lifecycle hooks
npm pkg set scripts.prebuild='npm run typecheck'
npm pkg set scripts.pretest='npm run lint'

# Composite scripts
npm pkg set scripts.validate='npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run build'
npm pkg set scripts.clean='rm -rf .next node_modules/.cache dist'

echo ""
echo "=== Updated Scripts ==="
npm run

faq.question: What lifecycle hooks does npm support?
faq.answer: npm supports pre and post hooks for any script. For 'npm run build', it runs prescripts in order: prebuild then build then postbuild. Built-in scripts like install, publish, and test also have lifecycle hooks like preinstall and postinstall.

faq.question: How do I pass arguments to npm scripts?
faq.answer: Use -- to pass arguments through: 'npm test -- --watch --verbose'. Everything after -- is appended to the script command. This works with any npm script.

faq.question: How do I make scripts cross-platform?
faq.answer: Install cross-env for environment variables and shx for shell commands. Use 'cross-env NODE_ENV=production' instead of 'NODE_ENV=production' and 'shx rm -rf dist' instead of 'rm -rf dist'.
